toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# Build ID: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-575db325f67a42aea44bad5e85c55135/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the ELF debug symbol format
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

echo "---"
# Also try downloading the library itself with symbols
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
Now I need to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames. The key information is:

- Library: `libmonosgen-2.0.so`
- Build ID: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

The frames I need to symbolicate are all the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames across all threads:

**Thread 7020 (main/crash thread):**
- #01 pc 0000000000222098
- #02 pc 00000000002241ac
- #03 pc 00000000001abc80


- #04 pc 000000000016de04

**Thread 7046 (SGen worker):**
- #02 pc 00000000002f4b20
- #03 pc 00000000002f4c84

**Thread 7048 (Finalizer):**
- #02 pc 00000000002c1a40

I need to locate the debug symbols for this library using the Build ID. I can try fetching them from a symbol server—Microsoft hosts debug symbols at a standard endpoint that I can query with this Build ID.

For ELF files with Build IDs, I should try the Microsoft symbol server using the standard URL format with the filename and Build ID, or look for the .dbg file directly. Once I have the symbols, I can use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve these memory addresses to actual function names and line numbers.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>&1; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# Build ID: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
SYMDIR="/tmp/sv-575db325f67a42aea44bad5e85c55135/symbols"
mkdir -p "$SYMDIR"

# Try the ELF debug symbol format
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/_.debug" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

echo "---"
# Also try downloading the library itself with symbols
curl -sSL -o "$SYMDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP status: %{http_code}\nSize: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

# Thread 7020 (main): 0x222098, 0x2241ac, 0x1abc80, 0x16de04
